Water Sample Code

This is an R Markdown Notebook. When you execute code within the notebook, the results appear beneath the code.

Try executing this chunk by clicking the Run button within the chunk or by placing your cursor inside it and pressing Ctrl+Shift+Enter.

START CODE

adding a clearance rate column to the data set water_sample_data

water_sample_data <- within(water_sample_data, clearance.rate <- ((volume/(1*time))*log(initial.conc/final.conc)))
print(water_sample_data)
# A tibble: 12 × 10
   mussel.Id mussel.Weight Mussel.length mussel.height mussel.width initial.conc final.conc volume  time clearance.rate
       <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>         <dbl>        <dbl>        <dbl>      <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl>
 1      3361          5.22           3.5           2            1.2        70833      11667    900     3           541.
 2      3349          7.64           4.1           2.4          1.3        60000      20000    900     3           330.
 3      3352         11.6            4.4           2.5          1.6        48333       5000    900     3           681.
 4      3340          5.34           3.4           2.1          1.1       114167      18333    900     3           549.
 5      3353          7.42           3.7           2.2          1.3       148333      21667    900     3           577.
 6      3357         11.6            4.4           2.7          1.5       135833       6667    900     3           904.
 7      3362          5.36           3.5           2.1          1.1       241667      10000    900     3           955.
 8      3363          7.1            3.7           2.2          1.4       178333      11667    900     3           818.
 9      3341         10.4            4             2.4          1.5       185833       8333    900     3           931.
10      3346          5.84           3.7           2.1          1.2       155833      21667    900     3           592.
11      3348          7.05           3.8           2.2          1.3       215000      20000    900     3           712.
12      3338         10.5            4.4           2.4          1.5       186667       6667    900     3          1000.
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(water_sample_data, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = clearance.rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  xlab("Mussel Weight (g)") + ylab("Clearance Rate (mL/H)")

ggplot(water_sample_data, aes(x = initial.conc, y = clearance.rate)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  xlab("Inital Algae Concentration (cells/mL)") + ylab("Clearance Rate (mL/H)")

library(dplyr)
summary_df_h0 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.zero, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.zero, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
summary_df_h1 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.one, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.one, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
summary_df_h2 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(hour.two, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(hour.two, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
summary_df_h3 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(final.three, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(final.three, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
summary_df <- bind_rows(
  summary_df_h0 %>% mutate(hour = 0),
  summary_df_h1 %>% mutate(hour = 1),
  summary_df_h2 %>% mutate(hour = 2),
  summary_df_h3 %>% mutate(hour = 3)
)
as.data.frame(summary_df)
plot_summary <- function(money,c){ 
  summary_df %>%
    filter(Treatment == money) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = hour, y = mean_val)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = c ) +
    ggtitle(money, "Algae starting concentration over time") +
    xlab("Time (hr)") + ylab("Algae concentration (cells/ml)") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)
}
plot_low <- plot_summary("Low", "red")
plot_medium <- plot_summary("Medium", "blue")
plot_high <- plot_summary("High", "green")
plot_highest <- plot_summary("Highest", "violet")
library(cowplot)
plot_grid(plot_low, plot_medium, plot_high, plot_highest)

 ggplot(summary_df, aes(x = hour, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE ) +
    ggtitle("Algae starting concentration over time") +
    xlab("Time (hr)") + ylab("Algae concentration (cells/ml") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)

CR_OT <- summary_df %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  transmute(1 = ((900/(1*1))*log(mean_val[hour == 0]/ mean_val[hour == 1])),
Error: unexpected '=' in:
"  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  transmute(1 ="
CR_OT <- unique(CR_OT)
library(tidyr)
CR_OT2 <- CR_OT %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("hr"),
    names_to = "interval",
    values_to = "clearance.rate"
  )
CR_OT2 <- CR_OT2 %>%
  mutate(interval = recode(interval,
                               "hr 0-1" = 1,
                               "hr 1-2" = 2,
                               "hr 2-3" = 3))
ggplot(CR_OT2, aes(x = interval, y = clearance.rate, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE) +
    ggtitle("Clearance rate over time") +
    xlab("Time interval") + ylab("Clearance rate (ml/hr)")

model <- lm(clearance.rate ~ mussel.Weight * Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
anova(model)
summary(model)
model <- lm(clearance.rate ~ mussel.Weight + Treatment, data = water_sample_1)
anova(model)
summary(model)
ggplot(water_sample_1, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = clearance.rate, colour = Treatment)) + geom_point() + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE)
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.overall <- ((volume/(1*time))*log(hour.zero/final.three)))
print(water_sample_1)

# ℹ 1 more variable: clearance.rate.overall <dbl>
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_1 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.zero/hour.one)))
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_2 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.one/hour.two)))
water_sample_1 <- within(water_sample_1, clearance.rate.interval_3 <- ((volume/(1*1))*log(hour.two/final.three)))
CR_interval1 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_1, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
  
CR_interval2 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
CR_interval3 <- water_sample_1 %>%
  group_by(Treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate.interval_3, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate.interval_3, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
print(water_sample_1)
summary_cr <- bind_rows(
  CR_interval1 %>% mutate(interval = 1),
  CR_interval2 %>% mutate(interval = 2),
  CR_interval3 %>% mutate(interval = 3)
)
ggplot(summary_cr, aes(x = interval, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("hour interval") + ylab("Clearnace Rate (ml/hr)") + 
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_val - se_val, ymax = mean_val + se_val), 
                                                                             width = 0.1,
                                                                             color = "black",
                                                                             linewidth = 0.5)

ggplot(summary_cr, aes(x = interval, y = mean_val, colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("Interval") + ylab("Clearance Rate (ml/hr)")

ggplot(water_sample_1, aes(x = mussel.Weight, y = log(clearance.rate), colour = Treatment)) + 
    geom_point() + 
    geom_line() +
    xlab("Mussel weight(g)") + ylab("log of Clearance Rate")

sum_cr <- water_sample_1%>%
  group_by(Treatment)%>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_val = mean(clearance.rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_val = sd(clearance.rate, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_val = sd_val / sqrt(n)
  )
  
m1 <- lm(log(clearance.rate) ~ log(mussel.Weight) + hour.zero, data = water_sample_1)
anova(m1)

  geom_col() + 
  ylab("Initial algae concentration (cells/ml)") +

                                                                             width = 0.1,

lm(formula = log(clearance.rate) ~ Treatment, data = water_sample_1)

Residuals:
    Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
-0.4069 -0.1902  0.0737  0.1446  0.3182 

Coefficients:
                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)        6.6423     0.1585  41.916 1.16e-10 ***
TreatmentHighest   0.1371     0.2241   0.612   0.5576    
TreatmentLow      -0.4375     0.2241  -1.952   0.0867 .  
TreatmentMedium   -0.1514     0.2241  -0.676   0.5183    
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 0.2745 on 8 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.4755,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.2788 
F-statistic: 2.418 on 3 and 8 DF,  p-value: 0.1415
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
set.seed(42)
dat <- tibble(
  animal_id = factor(1:12),
  concentration = factor(rep(c("C", "L", "M", "H"),each = 3),
                         levels = c("C", "L", "M", "H")),
  weight = runif(12,0.5, 3.0),
  clearance_rate = rnorm(12, mean = 5, sd = 1.5)
)%>%
  mutate(
    log_cr = log(clearance_rate),
    log_weight = log(weight)
  )
dat1 <- tibble(
  animal_id = water_sample_1$mussel.Id,
  concentration = water_sample_1$Treatment,
  weight = water_sample_1$mussel.Weight,
  clearance_rate = water_sample_1$clearance.rate
)%>%
  mutate(
    log_cr = log(clearance_rate),
    log_weight = log(weight)
  )
# ── Model 1: full — weight x concentration interaction ───────────────────────
mod1 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight * concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 2: additive — weight and concentration as main effects only ─────────
mod2 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 3: concentration only ───────────────────────────────────────────────
mod3 <- lm(log_cr ~ concentration, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 4: weight only ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mod4 <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight, data = dat1)
 
# ── Model 5: null ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mod5 <- lm(log_cr ~ 1, data = dat1)
# ── AIC overview ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AIC(mod1, mod2, mod3, mod4, mod5)
 
# ── Nested LRT comparisons ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
 
# Test weight x concentration interaction: is effect of weight on
# clearance rate different between concentration treatments?
anova(mod1, mod2)
Analysis of Variance Table

Model 1: log_cr ~ log_weight * concentration
Model 2: log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration
  Res.Df     RSS Df Sum of Sq      F Pr(>F)
1      4 0.34373                           
2      7 0.40129 -3 -0.057563 0.2233 0.8759
 
# Test weight term: does size explain variance after accounting for concentration?
anova(mod2, mod3)
Analysis of Variance Table

Model 1: log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration
Model 2: log_cr ~ concentration
  Res.Df     RSS Df Sum of Sq      F Pr(>F)
1      7 0.40129                           
2      8 0.60267 -1  -0.20138 3.5127  0.103
 
# Test concentration term: does treatment explain variance after weight?
anova(mod2, mod4)
Analysis of Variance Table

Model 1: log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration
Model 2: log_cr ~ log_weight
  Res.Df     RSS Df Sum of Sq      F  Pr(>F)  
1      7 0.40129                              
2     10 0.98770 -3  -0.58641 3.4097 0.08221 .
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1
# Update mod_best based on selection results above
mod_best <- mod2   # placeholder — update as needed
 
summary(mod_best)

Call:
lm(formula = log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration, data = dat1)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-0.40090 -0.12315  0.01573  0.13691  0.26352 

Coefficients:
                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)            5.7464     0.4976  11.547 8.23e-06 ***
log_weight             0.4429     0.2363   1.874   0.1030    
concentrationHighest   0.1494     0.1956   0.764   0.4699    
concentrationLow      -0.4483     0.1956  -2.292   0.0556 .  
concentrationMedium   -0.1600     0.1955  -0.818   0.4401    
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1

Residual standard error: 0.2394 on 7 degrees of freedom
Multiple R-squared:  0.6508,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.4512 
F-statistic: 3.261 on 4 and 7 DF,  p-value: 0.0825
anova(mod_best)
Analysis of Variance Table

Response: log_cr
              Df  Sum Sq  Mean Sq F value  Pr(>F)  
log_weight     1 0.16138 0.161379  2.8150 0.13728  
concentration  3 0.58641 0.195469  3.4097 0.08221 .
Residuals      7 0.40129 0.057328                  
---
Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1
 
# ── Check assumptions ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(mod_best)

 
# ── Post-hoc pairwise comparisons between concentration levels ────────────────
emmeans(mod_best, pairwise ~ concentration, adjust = "tukey")
$emmeans
 concentration emmean    SE df lower.CL upper.CL
 High            6.64 0.138  7     6.32     6.97
 Highest         6.79 0.138  7     6.47     7.12
 Low             6.20 0.138  7     5.87     6.52
 Medium          6.48 0.138  7     6.16     6.81

Confidence level used: 0.95 

$contrasts
 contrast         estimate    SE df t.ratio p.value
 High - Highest     -0.149 0.196  7  -0.764  0.8681
 High - Low          0.448 0.196  7   2.292  0.1890
 High - Medium       0.160 0.196  7   0.818  0.8442
 Highest - Low       0.598 0.196  7   3.051  0.0700
 Highest - Medium    0.309 0.196  7   1.580  0.4455
 Low - Medium       -0.288 0.195  7  -1.474  0.4984

P value adjustment: tukey method for comparing a family of 4 estimates 
 
# ── Plot clearance rate by concentration ─────────────────────────────────────
ggplot(dat1, aes(x = concentration, y = exp(log_cr))) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 4, colour = "red") +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar",
               width = 0.15, colour = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "Algae concentration treatment",
    y = "Clearance rate (mL/hr)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12)

dat1$concentration <- factor(dat1$concentration,
                             levels = c("Low", "Medium","High", "Highest"))
# Range of sample sizes to test
n_range <- seq(6, 60, by = 6)
 
power_results <- map_dbl(n_range, function(n) {
  # Simulate 500 datasets of size n with similar effect size to your data
  sims <- replicate(500, {
   sim_dat <- tibble(
  concentration = factor(
  rep(c("Low", "Medium","High", "Highest"), length.out = n),
  levels = c("Low", "Medium","High", "Highest")),
  log_weight = rnorm(n,
                     mean = mean(dat1$log_weight),
                     sd = sd(dat1$log_weight))
)

# Generate log clearance rate from your fitted model
mu <- predict(mod2, newdata = sim_dat)

sim_dat$log_cr <- rnorm(n,
                        mean = mu,
                        sd = sigma(mod2)
                        )
    mod_sim <- lm(log_cr ~ log_weight + concentration, data = sim_dat)
    anova(mod_sim)["concentration", "Pr(>F)"] < 0.05
  })
  mean(sims)
})
 
tibble(n = n_range, power = power_results) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n, y = power)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1, colour = "#2A9D8F") +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", colour = "#E63946") +
  annotate("text", x = max(n_range), y = 0.82, label = "80% power",
           hjust = 1, colour = "#E63946") +
  labs(x = "Sample size (n)", y = "Power") +
  theme_classic()

power_df <- tibble(
  n = n_range,
  power = power_results
)

power_df %>%
  filter(power >= 0.8) %>%
  slice(1)
power_df <- tibble(
  n = n_range,
  power = power_results
)

power_df %>%
  filter(power >= 0.9) %>%
  slice(1)
power_df <- tibble(
  n = n_range,
  power = power_results
)

power_df %>%
  filter(power >= 0.99) %>%
  slice(1)
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# MODEL DIAGNOSTICS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
 
library(tidyverse)
 
# ── 1. Base R diagnostic plots (residuals, QQ, scale-location, leverage) ──────
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))
plot(mod1, main = "Model 1: log_weight * concentration")
 
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(mod2, main = "Model 2: log_weight + concentration")
 
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(mod3, main = "Model 3: concentration only")
 
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(mod4, main = "Model 4: log_weight only")
 
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))   # reset

 
# ── 2. Residuals vs fitted for each model side by side ───────────────────────
diag_dat <- bind_rows(
  tibble(model = "mod1: weight * conc",
         fitted = fitted(mod1), resid = resid(mod1)),
  tibble(model = "mod2: weight + conc",
         fitted = fitted(mod2), resid = resid(mod2)),
  tibble(model = "mod3: conc only",
         fitted = fitted(mod3), resid = resid(mod3)),
  tibble(model = "mod4: weight only",
         fitted = fitted(mod4), resid = resid(mod4))
)
 
ggplot(diag_dat, aes(x = fitted, y = resid)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "red") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE, colour = "blue", linewidth = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ model, scales = "free", ncol = 2) +
  labs(x = "Fitted values", y = "Residuals") +
  theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
        strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey92", colour = NA))
 
ggsave("plots/residuals_vs_fitted.png", width = 10, height = 7,
       units = "in", dpi = 300, bg = "white")

1: Yes
2: No
yes
Enter an item from the menu, or 0 to exit
1
 
# ── 3. QQ plots for each model ────────────────────────────────────────────────
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

qqnorm(resid(mod1), main = "QQ: mod1 weight * conc"); qqline(resid(mod1))
qqnorm(resid(mod2), main = "QQ: mod2 weight + conc"); qqline(resid(mod2))
qqnorm(resid(mod3), main = "QQ: mod3 conc only");     qqline(resid(mod3))
qqnorm(resid(mod4), main = "QQ: mod4 weight only");   qqline(resid(mod4))
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
 
# ── 4. Summary statistics for each model ─────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Model 1: log_weight * concentration ──────────────\n")

── Model 1: log_weight * concentration ──────────────
cat("Residual df:", df.residual(mod1), "\n")
Residual df: 4 
cat("R-squared:  ", round(summary(mod1)$r.squared, 3), "\n")
R-squared:   0.701 
cat("AIC:        ", round(AIC(mod1), 2), "\n")
AIC:         9.42 
 
cat("\n── Model 2: log_weight + concentration ──────────────\n")

── Model 2: log_weight + concentration ──────────────
cat("Residual df:", df.residual(mod2), "\n")
Residual df: 7 
cat("R-squared:  ", round(summary(mod2)$r.squared, 3), "\n")
R-squared:   0.651 
cat("AIC:        ", round(AIC(mod2), 2), "\n")
AIC:         5.28 
 
cat("\n── Model 3: concentration only ──────────────────────\n")

── Model 3: concentration only ──────────────────────
cat("Residual df:", df.residual(mod3), "\n")
Residual df: 8 
cat("R-squared:  ", round(summary(mod3)$r.squared, 3), "\n")
R-squared:   0.476 
cat("AIC:        ", round(AIC(mod3), 2), "\n")
AIC:         8.16 
 
cat("\n── Model 4: log_weight only ─────────────────────────\n")

── Model 4: log_weight only ─────────────────────────
cat("Residual df:", df.residual(mod4), "\n")
Residual df: 10 
cat("R-squared:  ", round(summary(mod4)$r.squared, 3), "\n")
R-squared:   0.14 
cat("AIC:        ", round(AIC(mod4), 2), "\n")
AIC:         10.09 
 
# ── 5. Formal test for normality of residuals (Shapiro-Wilk) ─────────────────
# Note: with n = 12 this test has very low power — use as indicative only
cat("\n── Shapiro-Wilk normality test on residuals ─────────\n")

── Shapiro-Wilk normality test on residuals ─────────
cat("mod1:", shapiro.test(resid(mod1))$p.value, "\n")
mod1: 0.294243 
cat("mod2:", shapiro.test(resid(mod2))$p.value, "\n")
mod2: 0.8236262 
cat("mod3:", shapiro.test(resid(mod3))$p.value, "\n")
mod3: 0.5345873 
cat("mod4:", shapiro.test(resid(mod4))$p.value, "\n")
mod4: 0.2489925 
 
# ── 6. Check for influential observations (Cook's distance) ───────────────────
# With n = 12 any Cook's D > 1 is worth investigating
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))

plot(cooks.distance(mod2), type = "h", main = "Cook's distance — mod2",
     ylab = "Cook's D", xlab = "Observation")
abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
 
plot(cooks.distance(mod1), type = "h", main = "Cook's distance — mod1",
     ylab = "Cook's D", xlab = "Observation")
abline(h = 1, lty = 2, col = "red")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

 
# ── 7. Confidence intervals on parameter estimates ────────────────────────────
cat("\n── 95% CI on model parameters ───────────────────────\n")

── 95% CI on model parameters ───────────────────────
cat("\nmod1:\n"); print(round(confint(mod1), 3))

mod1:
                                 2.5 % 97.5 %
(Intercept)                      1.203  9.163
log_weight                      -1.233  2.675
concentrationHighest            -3.898  6.595
concentrationLow                -4.583  5.351
concentrationMedium             -5.082  4.970
log_weight:concentrationHighest -3.185  1.991
log_weight:concentrationLow     -2.833  2.013
log_weight:concentrationMedium  -2.508  2.400
cat("\nmod2:\n"); print(round(confint(mod2), 3))

mod2:
                      2.5 % 97.5 %
(Intercept)           4.570  6.923
log_weight           -0.116  1.002
concentrationHighest -0.313  0.612
concentrationLow     -0.911  0.014
concentrationMedium  -0.622  0.302
cat("\nmod3:\n"); print(round(confint(mod3), 3))

mod3:
                      2.5 % 97.5 %
(Intercept)           6.277  7.008
concentrationHighest -0.380  0.654
concentrationLow     -0.954  0.079
concentrationMedium  -0.668  0.365
cat("\nmod4:\n"); print(round(confint(mod4), 3))

mod4:
             2.5 % 97.5 %
(Intercept)  4.316  7.140
log_weight  -0.294  1.085

END CODE

Add a new chunk by clicking the Insert Chunk button on the toolbar or by pressing Ctrl+Alt+I.

When you save the notebook, an HTML file containing the code and output will be saved alongside it (click the Preview button or press Ctrl+Shift+K to preview the HTML file).

The preview shows you a rendered HTML copy of the contents of the editor. Consequently, unlike Knit, Preview does not run any R code chunks. Instead, the output of the chunk when it was last run in the editor is displayed.
